name: Orangutan
summary: Bornean orangutans are great apes that live in Asian rainforests. They climb with long arms, eat forest fruit, and spend years raising their young.
metaTitle: Orangutan Facts | Habitat, Diet, Nests & Photos
metaDescription: Discover Bornean orangutan facts with photos, rainforest habitat, fruit diet, infant life, and nest building. Learn where orangutans live and why they are...
searchIntents: how are orangutans related to humans

lifespan.captivity: Up to 50 years in human care

speed: Slow on ground; agile climber in trees
funFacts: Orangutans share about 97 percent of their DNA with humans.
The word orangutan means person of the forest in Malay.
Orangutans build a fresh leafy nest to sleep in almost every night.
A male orangutan's arm span can reach more than 7 feet.
Orangutans are mostly solitary, unlike chimpanzees and gorillas.
Infants stay with their mothers for up to eight years.

faq.question: What is an orangutan?
faq.answer: An orangutan is a great ape that lives in Asian rainforests. Orangutans are expert climbers with long red hair and very long arms.

faq.question: Where do orangutans live?
faq.answer: Bornean orangutans live only on the island of Borneo in Indonesia and Malaysia.

faq.question: What do orangutans eat?
faq.answer: Orangutans eat fruit, leaves, bark, flowers, and sometimes insects. Fruit is an important food when trees are fruiting.

faq.question: Are orangutans endangered?
faq.answer: Yes. Bornean orangutans are listed as Critically Endangered because rainforests are being cleared for palm oil and logging.

faq.question: How are orangutans related to humans?
faq.answer: Orangutans are great apes and are closely related to humans, gorillas, and chimpanzees.

faq.question: Why do orangutans build nests?
faq.answer: Orangutans build leafy nests in trees to sleep safely above the forest floor each night.

faq.question: Do orangutans live in groups?
faq.answer: Adult orangutans usually live alone, though mothers stay with their infants for many years.
